metadata.description: A curated, public subset of the Nearform skills registry.

plugins.name: prompt-engineering
plugins.description: Rules and frameworks for writing high-quality LLM system prompts. Apply when authoring, reviewing, or optimizing a system prompt, system message, or LLM instructions.

plugins.name: clean-architecture
plugins.description: Enforces Clean Architecture layering for TypeScript factory-based codebases. Use when creating, refactoring, or reviewing handlers, services, domains, and repositories.

plugins.name: security-advisory-review
plugins.description: Assess security advisories against a Git repository by creating an isolated worktree, building a PoC exploit, and validating whether the described vulnerability is real and exploitable. Use when given a CVE, GitHub Security Advisory, or vulnerability description to verify.

plugins.name: test-automation-guidelines
plugins.description: Core principles for writing deterministic, reliable, and maintainable automated tests across frameworks. Use when writing, reviewing, or maintaining automated tests.

plugins.name: test-jira-cases
plugins.description: Generate reviewer-ready test cases from Jira tickets using Jira MCP. Use when a Jira issue, story, or epic needs coverage drafted for user review.

plugins.name: test-bdd
plugins.description: Behaviour-Driven Development practices for declarative Gherkin and thin, well-structured step definitions. Use when writing or reviewing BDD/Cucumber feature files and step definitions, delegating framework mechanics.

plugins.name: test-cypress
plugins.description: Cypress-specific best practices for reliable, idiomatic end-to-end tests. Use when writing or reviewing Cypress tests.

plugins.name: test-k6
plugins.description: k6-specific best practices for performance tests, including load, stress, spike, and soak testing. Use when writing or reviewing k6 performance tests.

plugins.name: test-pact
plugins.description: Pact-specific best practices for consumer and provider contract tests. Use when writing or reviewing Pact tests.

plugins.name: test-playwright
plugins.description: Playwright-specific best practices for reliable, parallel-safe end-to-end tests. Use when writing or reviewing Playwright tests.

plugins.name: test-review
plugins.description: Review automated tests against core and framework-specific testing standards. Diagnoses issues and produces a verdict; recommends test-refactor for structural changes. Detects the framework used and applies the matching framework's review patterns.

plugins.name: test-refactor
plugins.description: Refactor automated tests to improve maintainability, readability, and organization without changing behaviour. Detects the framework used and applies the matching framework's refactoring patterns.

plugins.name: test-webdriverio
plugins.description: WebdriverIO-specific best practices for stable and maintainable tests. Use when writing or reviewing WebdriverIO tests.

plugins.name: handoff
plugins.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document so a fresh agent can continue the work in a new session. Use when splitting scope across sessions, spawning prototype or side tasks, or passing context between agents.

plugins.name: understand-codebase
plugins.description: Build an interlinked markdown knowledge graph of an unfamiliar codebase and explore it. Adaptively provisions only what you ask for: knowledge notes, a Quartz graph viewer, a query interface, or incremental git-hook re-analysis. Use when onboarding to a new or undocumented project.

plugins.name: nearform-sql
plugins.description: Use this skill when writing, reviewing, or refactoring database queries with @nearform/sql — Nearform's tagged-template library that produces SQL-injection-safe parameterized queries for pg, mysql, and mysql2. Covers installation, the SQL tag (.text/.sql/.values/.debug), helpers (glue, map, unsafe, quoteIdent), Fastify integration, dynamic/bulk queries, and security best practices.

plugins.name: init-python-project
plugins.description: Scaffold a new Python project following modern best practices — uv by default (with pip/venv, pipenv, or manual fallbacks), plus ruff, mypy, pre-commit, and pytest. Use when starting, creating, or bootstrapping a new Python project.

plugins.name: brevity
plugins.description: Compress text to its load-bearing minimum without losing meaning. Inspired by Giuseppe Ungaretti.
